info.name: The California Pickle — Backend API
info.description:
Complete API collection for The California Pickle e-commerce backend.

**Setup:**
1. Set `baseUrl` variable to your server (default: http://localhost:4200)
2. Run `Login` first — auth cookie is set automatically
3. After creating a product/order, IDs are saved automatically to collection variables

**Auth:** Cookie-based (httpOnly). Postman/Requestly handles cookies automatically after Login.

exec: // Cookie is set automatically by Postman/Requestly cookie jar

request.description: Login as admin. Sets accessToken + refreshToken cookies automatically.
Uses refreshToken cookie to get a new accessToken. No body needed.
Clears auth cookies. Requires active session.
Sends a password reset email. Rate limited to 5 per hour.
Reset password using the token from the email link. Token expires in 15 minutes.
Returns the authenticated admin's profile. Requires auth cookie.
Public — returns the currently active product shown to customers.
Admin — returns all non-deleted products.
Admin — returns soft-deleted products available for restore.

request.description:
Admin — creates a new product. Always created as inactive (upcoming). Use Activate to go live.
Saves productId to collection variable automatically.
Admin — makes this product live on the storefront. Deactivates all others atomically. Blocks if all variants are UPCOMING.
Admin — update product fields or variant details (price, stock, weight, dimensions, images).
Admin — soft deletes (archives) a product. Cannot delete the active product or the last product.
Admin — restores a soft-deleted product back to inactive state. Does NOT auto-activate.

formdata.description: Select a single image file (jpg/png/webp, max 10MB)

request.description: Admin — uploads a single image to Cloudinary. Returns { url, publicId }.
Admin — uploads up to 5 images at once. Returns { images: [{ url, publicId }] }.

Public — call this when customer enters their address on the checkout form.
Returns live carrier rates from Shippo sorted cheapest first.
Save the rateId and amount from the rate the customer selects — you'll need both for checkout.

⚠️ Replace REPLACE_WITH_VARIANT_ID with a real variant _id from the product.

exec: console.log('Open this URL in browser to complete payment');

request.description:
Public — creates a pending order and returns a Stripe checkout URL.

Flow:
1. First call `Get Shipping Rates` to get rateId + amount
2. Fill in variantId, shippoRateId, and shippingCost from the rate customer selected
3. Open the returned checkoutUrl in browser to complete payment
4. orderId is saved to collection variable automatically

⚠️ Replace variantId, shippoRateId, and shippingCost with real values.

item.name: Get Order by Session ID (Public Receipt)

request.description:
Public — returns masked order info for the post-payment receipt page.
Stripe redirects customer here after checkout. sessionId comes from the Stripe session.
Admin — paginated list of all orders sorted newest first.
Admin — returns all pending/failed orders for the manual follow-up call list. Use this to see who hasn't paid.

query.description: Customer email to look up order history

request.description: Admin — returns all paid orders for a specific customer email. Use in dashboard customer detail view.

Admin — manually sends an abandoned cart reminder email to the customer after a call.
If the Stripe session expired, automatically generates a fresh checkout session and sends the new link.
Blocks if order is already paid.

Admin — update order fulfillment status.

Valid transitions:
  processing → shipped or cancelled
  shipped → delivered
  delivered → (terminal)
  cancelled → (terminal)

Blocks if order is not paid. Blocks invalid transitions.
Cancelled is admin-only emergency use (e.g. out of stock after payment).

item.name: Stripe Webhook (Stripe only — do not call manually)

header.description: Stripe signs every webhook. Do not set manually.

request.description:
⚠️ THIS IS CALLED BY STRIPE ONLY — not by your frontend.

For local testing use Stripe CLI:
  stripe listen --forward-to localhost:4200/api/payments/webhook

Stripe events handled:
  - checkout.session.completed → marks paid, deducts stock, purchases Shippo label, sends emails
  - checkout.session.expired  → marks failed
Admin — returns current store settings including the ship-from address used by Shippo.

Admin — update the warehouse ship-from address. This is used by Shippo for all shipping rate calculations and label purchases.

⚠️ Update this with the real California address before going live.
